notes: Stalder 2016 CAR expert consensus methodology; baseline sample taken in supine position immediately upon waking before getting out of bed. Numerical cutoffs are conservative central range; needs_clinical_lab_confirmation against specific assay manufacturer reference.
Stalder 2016 CAR consensus; t+30min post-wake sample is peak of typical CAR. Healthy CAR shows ~50-100% rise from t0 baseline. needs_clinical_lab_confirmation.

Stalder 2022 CAR consensus update — methodology row, not numerical reference. Establishes 4-point sampling protocol (0, 30, 45, 60 min post-wake) and authentication via objective wake-time (electronic monitoring). Use with PSN-CORT-001 + PSN-CORT-002 numerical reference rows.

Clow 2010 Int Rev Neurobiol — CAR-in-context framework. Establishes that CAR is mechanistically distinct from later-day diurnal cortisol decline; both should be measured for full HPA-axis assessment. Methodology row, not numerical.

Wüst 2000 — twin study showing genetic component to free cortisol awakening response. Family-history modifier: a parent with documented blunted/exaggerated CAR raises within-family-cluster prior on the same pattern in the child. Methodology row.

Frederiksen 2024 Clin Chim Acta — sex- and age-specific reference intervals for 16 steroid metabolites quantified simultaneously by LC-MS/MS. Reference paper for harmonized steroid measurement; cortisol reference intervals stratified by age + sex available in supplementary data. Cite for serum cortisol when measured.
